article_meta.title: Christmas travelers left stranded as airports see mass flight cancelations

reasoning: The summary avoids the article’s sensational framing and uses neutral, attributed language. It does not import loaded terms like “chaos” or “ho-ho-horrible,” sticks to verifiable figures, and balances details across regions and sources.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs and phrasing (e.g., “caused major disruptions”) instead of the article’s dramatic language.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All figures and assertions are attributed to FlightAware, FAA, or officials; no unsupported claims.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments presented as fact; qualifiers and attributions are used appropriately.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids emotive phrases like “chaos” or “ho-ho-horrible,” maintaining a measured tone.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or derogatory characterizations.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: Does not infer motives or feelings; reports observed impacts and quotes in aggregate.
custom_reasoning.slant: Event coverage with no competing ‘sides’; regional impacts are presented proportionally.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes East and West Coast impacts, emergency declarations, and prior-day metrics present in the article.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives like “major” and “widespread” are factual descriptors, not value-laden.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Reflects the negative nature of the event without amplifying it beyond the source.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official and data sources evenly; no preference for elites or grassroots.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Clearly more neutral than the source’s sensational framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Represents all cited sources (FlightAware, FAA, Fox Weather, NY Post) and the outlet; proportions match article emphasis.
